verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 is canonically ER-luminal but undergoes documented stress-induced translocation to the cell surface outer leaflet under ER stress, viral infection, and in cancer cells. During these states, surface HSPA5 functions as a viral receptor, co-receptor, and immune modulator. The protein body itself is transported to the PM via its own translocation mechanism, not via reversible recruitment by another surface protein, qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
